commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the root node-domexception override from the @nolyfill alias to node-domexception 1.0.0, updates override guard tests, and regenerates root pnpm/npm dependency metadata.

PR surface: Source -3, Tests 0, Config +5, Other -21. Total -19 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for a source-backed path: current main and v2026.6.10 still use the implicated override, npm lists no node-domexception@1.0.28, and the PR screenshots show the reported install failure. I did not run a fresh source install in this read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A repair worker can narrow the branch and regenerate valid dependency metadata, but maintainers/security still need to approve the final dependency graph.

target.title: Fix issue#95927:Install bug:No matching version found for node-domexception@1.0.28
target.body: Closes #95927 ## What Problem This Solves Fixes an issue(https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/95927) where users cloning and installing OpenClaw from source would encounter a hard pnpm install failure due to an unresolvable node-domexception@1.0.28 dependency version, completely blocking the build and development workflow. This error is triggered when downloaded extensions include plugins that depend on gaxios (such as the Google Chat plugin), as this introduces gaxios@7.1.5 and pulls in the node-domexception sub-dependency chain for the first time. ## Why This Change Was Made Adds a node-domexception: ^1.0.0 override to the workspace root so that pnpm resolves the dependency to the existing 1.0.0 release, aligning with the ^1.0.0 constraint already declared by fetch-blob. This fixes resolution failures triggered by gaxios-based plugins without upgrading fetch-blob or node-fetch, as both remain tied to the node-domexception 1.x line for CommonJS compatibility. ## User Impact Users can now successfully install and build OpenClaw from source even when their selected extensions include plugins that depend on gaxios (such as the Google Chat plugin). Previously, this workflow would fail with an ERR_PNPM_NO_MATCHING_VERSION error when downloaded extensions include such plugins, blocking the build entirely; after this fix, pnpm install && pnpm build && pnpm ui:build completes cleanly regardless of such plugins are enabled. ## Evidence before fixing: after fixing: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile: pnpm: build: pnpm: ui build: ## Root Cause: Unresolvable node-domexception Dependency ## Error Scope: all 153 workspace projects ✓ Lockfile passes supply-chain policies (1360 entries in 17s) [ERR_PNPM_NO_MATCHING_VERSION] No matching version found for node-domexception@1.0.28 while fetching it from https://registry.npmjs.org/ This error happened while installing the dependencies of @google/genai@2.7.0 at google-auth-library@10.7.0 at gaxios@7.1.5 at node-fetch@3.3.2 at fetch-blob@3.2.0 The latest release of node-domexception is "2.0.2". ## History Issue https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/60338 was previously fixed by pinning node-domexception to 1.0.28 in package.json. That version is no longer available on the npm registry. The package has since been deprecated by its author; only four versions remain: 1.0.0, 2.0.0, 2.0.1, and 2.0.2. ## Trigger The error appears when downloaded extensions include plugins that depend on gaxios—such as the Google Chat plugin. The first install succeeds without these plugins. The second install pulls in gaxios@7.1.5, which introduces the node-fetch → fetch-blob → node-domexception chain for the first time. ## Why Downgrade Is Not Viable fetch-blob@3.1.4 introduced the node-domexception requirement to fix a critical double-slice bug for filesystem-backed blobs. node-fetch@3.3.2 depends on fetch-blob@^3.1.4 specifically to include this fix. Downgrading either package would reintroduce the bug. ## The Fix fetch-blob itself declares node-domexception: ^1.0.0. While 2.0.2 appears to work in local testing, it is ESM-only and may introduce compatibility issues with the CommonJS-based fetch-blob 3.x line. The safest resolution is to add node-domexception: 1.0.0 to pnpm-workspace.yaml, aligning with fetch-blob's own constraint. The package.json override was already removed from main in favor of workspace-level configuration, so pnpm-workspace.yaml is the correct location for this fix. ## For the Review The errors reported by ```Dependency Guard / dependency-guard (pull_request_target) ``` are triggered by the modification of the node-domexception version in this PR, and do not reflect actual critical problems. I'm also puzzled by the ```CI / check-shrinkwrap (pull_request) ``` failure. The command node scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.mjs --all --check passes locally on my machine, but after pushing to the PR branch, GitHub CI fails with: `plain Run set -euo pipefail $ node scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.mjs --all --check generated npm-shrinkwrap.json violates workspace overrides: node_modules/cliui locked 8.0.1, expected 6.0.0; node_modules/wrap-ansi locked 7.0.0, expected 6.2.0; node_modules/y18n locked 5.0.8, expected 4.0.3; node_modules/yargs-parser locked 21.1.1, expected 18.1.3 [ELIFECYCLE] Command failed with exit code 1. Error: Process completed with exit code 1.` And here is my pass evidence on my machine: I've spent several hours on this, including aligning pnpm versions and double-checking these dependency versions, but I'm still at a loss. I'll try to find some free time (if any) to keep investigating, but I'd greatly appreciate any insights or assistance from maintainers or other contributors. Thanks in advance!
target.author: jnzhang233
